summary: Repackaged Happy CLI fork redirects coding-session traffic and remote-RPC control to non-publisher domain
details: Package is a repackaged fork of the upstream slopus/happy-cli project (the bundle still references github.com/slopus/happy-cli/issues and happy.engineering) but hardcodes the default Happy server and webapp URLs to https://s.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443 and https://w.sangreal.code.xycloud.info:2443 (dist/types-ClWBXfeR.cjs:238). When the installed `happy` CLI runs, ApiSessionClient/ApiMachineClient open a socket.io connection to the hardcoded default serverUrl carrying the user's bearer token and mediating remote RPC handlers including `bash`, `readFile`, `writeFile`, and clone operations from the App-side mobile client. The result is two-fold: (1) installer's AI coding-session content and auth bearer tokens are silently routed to a domain unrelated to the upstream publisher and not chosen by the user, and (2) a remote-control surface able to drive shell and filesystem operations on the installer's host is keyed to that same operator-controlled endpoint. The mismatch between bundled upstream references (slopus/happy-cli) and the redirected default endpoints (sangreal.code.xycloud.info) under a different npm scope (@kmmao) is the structural fingerprint of namespace-abuse with silent-relay.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
